title: Cloudflare Bot Management Score Distribution and Challenge Pass Rate (`cloudflare:json`)
dataSources: Cloudflare Logpush JSON delivered to Splunk HEC/S3 with `sourcetype=cloudflare:json` (HTTP requests, firewall, Workers, DNS, Access as configured)
app: Cloudflare App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4501) + Logpush JSON (`cloudflare:json`)
spl:
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| spath BotScore
| spath BotScoreSrc
| bin BotScore span=10
| stats count by BotScore
| eventstats sum(count) as tot
| eval pct=round(100*count/tot,2)
| sort BotScore
description: Bot scores quantify automated abuse versus humans. A collapsing median score or falling challenge pass rates indicates credential stuffing or scraping.
value: Protects login and checkout flows while avoiding over-blocking legitimate automation partners.
implementation: Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.
visualization: Histogram (BotScore), line chart (median score), single value (automated request %).
grandmaExplanation: We read your Cloudflare edge logs in one place so attacks, misconfigurations, and outages are obvious early.
